deep_dive.title: TaskTok: Delving into Task Tokens for Task-driven Image Restoration
deep_dive.abstract: While traditional image restoration focuses on perceptual quality, Task-Driven Image Restoration (TDIR) aims to maximize the performance of downstream high-level vision tasks. Recent approaches leveraging generative priors have shown promise for TDIR; however, they typically suffer from computational inefficiency and potential semantic alteration by indiscriminately updating all latent tokens. In this paper, we posit that not all visual information is equally important for machine perception. Through an analysis of the latent token space, we observe that task-relevant cues are unevenly distributed across the token sequence, exhibiting index-wise specialization. This suggests that selectively refining a subset of tokens can be sufficient for task-driven objectives. Leveraging this insight, we propose TaskTok, a novel framework that selectively restores only task-relevant tokens via a learnable token switch and a lightweight token refinement module. Extensive experiments across image classification, semantic segmentation, and object detection demonstrate that TaskTok significantly enhances task performance with high computational efficiency. The source code is available at https://github.com/jimmy9704/TaskTok
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Task-driven image restoration using generative priors
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用生成先验的任务驱动图像修复
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用生成先验的任务驱动图像修复
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用生成先验的任务驱动图像修复
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes TaskTok, which selectively refines task-relevant tokens in latent space for efficient task-driven image restoration using generative priors. It analyzes token specialization and shows significant computational savings without compromising downstream task performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对任务驱动图像修复中计算效率低和语义改变问题，该论文提出TaskTok方法。通过分析潜在令牌空间，发现任务相关线索在令牌序列中呈索引专业化分布，因此仅需精炼子集令牌即可满足下游任务需求。实验表明该方法显著降低计算开销，同时保持或提升机器感知性能，为高效视觉修复提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对任务驱动图像修复中计算效率低和语义改变问题，该论文提出TaskTok方法。通过分析潜在令牌空间，发现任务相关线索在令牌序列中呈索引专业化分布，因此仅需精炼子集令牌即可满足下游任务需求。实验表明该方法显著降低计算开销，同时保持或提升机器感知性能，为高效视觉修复提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models

deep_dive.title: LogicIR: Logic Gate Networks for Image Restoration
deep_dive.abstract: Image restoration aims to reconstruct high-quality images from degraded low-quality inputs. As the computational demands of image restoration models continue to rise, there is growing interest in lightweight architectures optimized for fast and efficient inference. Logic gate networks (LGNs), which operate using fundamental logic operations such as NAND and XOR, have recently emerged as a promising direction for achieving highly efficient computation. However, their potential remains largely untapped in the domain of image restoration. In this work, we introduce LogicIR, the first LGN specifically designed for image restoration tasks. LogicIR incorporates a UNet-inspired architecture composed entirely of logic gates. In addition, we propose a differentiable bit decoding layer and an index shuffling mechanism that improves information propagation across logic gates. Experimental results across multiple image restoration benchmarks demonstrate that LogicIR achieves strong performance with significantly reduced computational cost, establishing LogicIR as a viable and efficient alternative for image restoration. The source code is available at https://github.com/jimmy9704/LogicIR
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Introduces the first logic gate network for image restoration tasks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 引入首个用于图像修复任务的逻辑门网络
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 引入首个用于图像修复任务的逻辑门网络
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 引入首个用于图像修复任务的逻辑门网络
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LogicIR is the first logic gate network designed for image restoration, using a UNet-inspired architecture made entirely of logic gates and a differentiable bit decoding layer to achieve efficient and fast inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 图像修复旨在从退化输入重建高质量图像，但现有模型计算开销大。本文提出LogicIR，首个专为图像修复任务设计的逻辑门网络。它采用UNet启发的架构，完全由NAND和XOR等逻辑门组成，并引入可微比特解码层以优化梯度传播。实验表明，LogicIR在保持修复质量的同时，实现了极高的推理效率。这一工作拓展了逻辑门网络的应用边界，为图像修复领域的轻量化模型提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 图像修复旨在从退化输入重建高质量图像，但现有模型计算开销大。本文提出LogicIR，首个专为图像修复任务设计的逻辑门网络。它采用UNet启发的架构，完全由NAND和XOR等逻辑门组成，并引入可微比特解码层以优化梯度传播。实验表明，LogicIR在保持修复质量的同时，实现了极高的推理效率。这一工作拓展了逻辑门网络的应用边界，为图像修复领域的轻量化模型提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Diffusion-Driven State Space Models
deep_dive.abstract: In many domains, practitioners seek models that produce accurate forecasts while faithfully capturing latent system dynamics. Existing approaches typically sacrifice one of these goals: deep state space models often assume Gaussian latent transitions, limiting fit and forecasting, while diffusion models are highly expressive but lack principled inference for the underlying dynamics. To combine the strengths of both, we introduce the Diffusion-Driven State Space Model (DDSSM), which replaces the conventional Gaussian transition distribution with a diffusion model. Our DDSSM resolves the open problem of how to jointly train an autoencoder and a diffusion model on sequential data, thereby extending the literature on latent diffusion models for time series. Moreover, we find that the DDSSM empirically outperforms a state-of-the-art deep SSM at fitting and forecasting a simulated time series with multimodal transitions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Combines diffusion models with state space models for time series forecasting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将扩散模型与状态空间模型结合用于时间序列预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将扩散模型与状态空间模型结合用于时间序列预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将扩散模型与状态空间模型结合用于时间序列预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces DDSSM, replacing Gaussian transitions in state space models with diffusion, enabling expressive dynamics modeling and improved forecasting for sequential data.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为了解决现有深度状态空间模型假设高斯潜变量过渡导致拟合不足与扩散模型缺乏潜动态推断的问题，提出扩散驱动状态空间模型（DDSSM），用扩散模型替代传统高斯过渡分布，实现序列数据的联合自编码器与扩散模型训练，提升了时间序列预测精度和动态捕捉能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为了解决现有深度状态空间模型假设高斯潜变量过渡导致拟合不足与扩散模型缺乏潜动态推断的问题，提出扩散驱动状态空间模型（DDSSM），用扩散模型替代传统高斯过渡分布，实现序列数据的联合自编码器与扩散模型训练，提升了时间序列预测精度和动态捕捉能力。

deep_dive.title: SeFi-Image: A Text-to-Image Foundation Model with Semantic-First Diffusion
deep_dive.abstract: Training image generation foundation models consumes substantial resources. Previous methods have attempted to leverage semantic guidance to accelerate the training process, yet their experiments were only conducted on simple datasets such as ImageNet, at low resolutions, and with small-scale models. In this paper, we propose SeFi-Image, a text-to-image foundation model built upon semantic-first diffusion, a novel latent diffusion modeling paradigm. We instantiate SeFi-Image at three model scales, 1B, 2B, and 5B parameters, enabling systematic study of scaling behavior and flexible deployment under varying compute budgets. Notably, our largest 5B model was trained with merely 125K A800 GPU hours, corresponding to roughly 10-20% of the training compute used by Z-Image. However, it achieves results comparable to or even superior to Qwen-Image and Z-Image. Despite this modest training compute, SeFi-Image achieves strong performance on a wide range of benchmarks, including GenEval, DPG, LongTextBench, OneIG, and CVTG-2K. Moreover, we provide DMD2-distilled few-step turbo variants for each model scale to accommodate diverse hardware constraints and latency requirements. We publicly release our code, weights and hope this work offers the community useful insights into semantic-guided diffusion modeling for T2I generation, while also providing practical and readily deployable model options.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes a text-to-image foundation model with semantic-first diffusion
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于语义优先扩散的文本到图像基础模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于语义优先扩散的文本到图像基础模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于语义优先扩散的文本到图像基础模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SeFi-Image, a text-to-image foundation model with semantic-first diffusion, scaling from 1B to 5B parameters, trained efficiently in only 125K A800 GPU hours for the 5B model, showing scaling behavior under limited compute.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 提出SeFi-Image，一种基于语义优先扩散的文本到图像基础模型，通过三种规模（1B、2B、5B参数）实例化以研究缩放行为。仅用125K A800 GPU小时训练5B模型，约占同类模型训练开销的10-20%，实现了高效的扩展性。该方法为资源高效的图像生成基础模型训练提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 提出SeFi-Image，一种基于语义优先扩散的文本到图像基础模型，通过三种规模（1B、2B、5B参数）实例化以研究缩放行为。仅用125K A800 GPU小时训练5B模型，约占同类模型训练开销的10-20%，实现了高效的扩展性。该方法为资源高效的图像生成基础模型训练提供了新范式。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索图像修复、超分辨率与生成扩散模型领域的代表性论文，涵盖基础模型、算法改进、训练策略、评测与应用, especially work that connects or combines: I; Generation Models; Image Restoration & Super Resolution; Diffusion Models; Image Editing Models; Generative Models; Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models.

deep_dive.title: TMP: Tree-structured Mixed-policy Pruning for Large-scale Image Generation and Editing
deep_dive.abstract: Modern image generation model rapidly grows their sizes to meet high-fidelity image synthesis. However, they gradually become unaffordable for their enormous parameter consumption and computation budget that lead to massive resources requirement and gpu memory footprint. In this paper, we propose TMP, the first Tree-structured Mixed-policy Pruning framework that generalizes prevalent image tasks (T2I and TI2I) and architectures (Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) and Diffusion transformer (DiT)). It could be applied to the step-distilled models and contribute as the last stage. We perform experiments upon current open-sourced SOTA HunyuanImage-3.0 instruct and a popular efficient model Z-Image turbo. The proposed pruning framework manages to compress HunyuanImage 3.0 from 80B to 20B parameters at 75% reduction ratio, sacrificing limited generation quality. We also optimize to enable the inference of the pruned 20B version of HunyuanImage 3.0 on a single 24GB 4090 GPU by engineering skills. The inference script and model weight have been integrated into the existing HunyuanImage3.0 open-source github and huggingface repository. Besides, we prove the efficacy of TMP by compressing Z-Image turbo from 6B to 4B (33% reduction) with negligible degradation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: First tree-structured mixed-policy pruning framework for image generation and editing models like DiT and MoE
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 首个面向图像生成与编辑模型（如DiT和MoE）的树结构混合策略剪枝框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 首个面向图像生成与编辑模型（如DiT和MoE）的树结构混合策略剪枝框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 首个面向图像生成与编辑模型（如DiT和MoE）的树结构混合策略剪枝框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: To reduce the enormous resource consumption of large image generation models, the paper proposes TMP, a pruning framework that generalizes across tasks (T2I, TI2I) and architectures (MoE, DiT), significantly compressing models like HunyuanImage-3.0 while maintaining performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大规模图像生成模型资源消耗巨大的问题，提出TMP剪枝框架，可通用于文生图和图像编辑等任务及MoE和DiT架构，在HunyuanImage-3.0等模型上实现大幅压缩且保持性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大规模图像生成模型资源消耗巨大的问题，提出TMP剪枝框架，可通用于文生图和图像编辑等任务及MoE和DiT架构，在HunyuanImage-3.0等模型上实现大幅压缩且保持性能。

deep_dive.title: Safe Autoregressive Image Generation with Iterative Self-Improving Codebooks
deep_dive.abstract: Unlike diffusion-based models that operate in continuous latent spaces, autoregressive unified multimodal models produce images by sequentially predicting discretized visual tokens. These tokens are derived from a codebook that maps embeddings to quantized visual patterns. The language-like architecture enables unified multimodal models to effectively capture text conditional information for generation, making them promising for text-to-image tasks. This also raises an interesting question: how safe are the images generated in such an autoregressive way? In this work, we propose iterative self-improving codebooks for safe autoregressive generation. We leverage the understanding and judgment capabilities of the unified multimodal model itself to identify unsafe generated images without human annotation. Subsequently, the inherent representations in the codebook are fixed to eliminate harmful mappings. Our method comprises two steps: first, we use the unified model to identify unsafe generations and construct corresponding harmful and safe image-text pairs. These pairs are used to construct the Harmful Space and guide updates to the codebook, thereby eliminating harmful outputs. Second, we perform adaptive fine-tuning on the codebook within the harmless space using safe image-text pairs to ensure the quality of generated images. These two steps are repeated until no further improvement is observed, producing a safety-enhanced model codebook. Without additional external feedback, the safety of models is improved iteratively.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes safe autoregressive image generation using iterative codebook improvement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出迭代自改进码本的安全自回归图像生成方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出迭代自改进码本的安全自回归图像生成方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出迭代自改进码本的安全自回归图像生成方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses safety in autoregressive image generation by iteratively refining visual codebooks using the model's own judgment, ensuring generated images are safer.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自回归统一多模态模型在文生图任务中生成不安全图像的风险，提出迭代自改进码本方法，利用模型自身的理解与评判能力迭代优化视觉码本，从而在自回归生成过程中有效过滤不安全内容，提升生成图像的安全性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对自回归统一多模态模型在文生图任务中生成不安全图像的风险，提出迭代自改进码本方法，利用模型自身的理解与评判能力迭代优化视觉码本，从而在自回归生成过程中有效过滤不安全内容，提升生成图像的安全性。

quick_skim.title: Modular Diffusion Models for Structured Visual Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Traditional supervised methods for structured visual recognition tasks -- such as object detection, segmentation, and scene graph generation -- often produce deterministic, fixed outputs, limiting their ability to capture the inherent uncertainty in complex visual scenes. As a consequence, such point estimates are unable to capture the prediction uncertainty (or multi modality) intrinsic to these problems, often arising from natural ambiguities (e.g., ambiguity in size of partially occluded objects, local ambiguity of exact segmentation boundary, etc.) as well as noise and sparsity of training data. To address this limitation, we present Modular Diffusion Models (MDMs), a simple and novel framework that learns a distribution over structured outputs for a given input image. MDMs decompose the diffusion process into distinct, task-specific modules, each focused on capturing a different aspect of the structured information space, such as object categories, spatial locations, and inter-object relationships. This modular design allows each component to be learned independently, with seamless integration at inference without additional training. Furthermore, the modularity of MDMs enables the diffusion process to easily operate over the heterogeneous output space common in many structured learning tasks (e.g., a continuous bounding boxes and discrete class labels). Experimental results over three distinct structured tasks -- object detection, instance segmentation, and scene graph generation -- highlight the benefits of our proposed framework.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Applies diffusion models to structured prediction tasks like detection and segmentation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将扩散模型应用于检测、分割等结构化预测任务
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将扩散模型应用于检测、分割等结构化预测任务
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将扩散模型应用于检测、分割等结构化预测任务
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MDM framework that uses diffusion models to capture uncertainty in structured visual outputs, improving upon deterministic models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统监督方法在目标检测、分割等结构化视觉任务中仅能提供点估计、无法刻画不确定性的局限，提出模块化扩散模型（MDM），通过学习输出分布生成多样化合理预测，增强了模型对遮挡、模糊等歧义的处理能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对传统监督方法在目标检测、分割等结构化视觉任务中仅能提供点估计、无法刻画不确定性的局限，提出模块化扩散模型（MDM），通过学习输出分布生成多样化合理预测，增强了模型对遮挡、模糊等歧义的处理能力。

quick_skim.title: MGI: Member vs Generated Inference
quick_skim.abstract: As generative models increasingly produce samples that are indistinguishable from human-created content, it becomes difficult to determine whether a given data point was part of a model's natural training set or was generated by the model itself, especially when models memorize and reproduce training data. We formalize this challenge as Member vs Generated Inference (MGI): given a sample and a target generative model, infer whether the sample is a true training member or a generated output of that model. Focusing on image generation, we show that existing membership inference methods systematically misclassify generated samples as training members, while attribution-based methods often misclassify true members as generated. This failure arises because both approaches rely on likelihood-related signals that are similarly elevated for training examples and for the model's own outputs. To address MGI, we propose Data Circuit Breaker (DCB), a three-stage method that combines complementary signals from a generative model's autoencoder and latent generator to distinguish training members from generated samples. Across multiple generative models, including image autoregressive and diffusion models, DCB consistently addresses the shortcomings of membership inference and attribution methods, remains effective even when models reproduce near-duplicates of training samples, and generalizes to challenging model derivative settings in which new models are trained on generated data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Focuses on distinguishing training vs generated samples in generative models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 聚焦于区分生成模型中的训练样本与生成样本
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 聚焦于区分生成模型中的训练样本与生成样本
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 聚焦于区分生成模型中的训练样本与生成样本
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Formalizes Member vs Generated Inference (MGI) for generative image models, showing existing methods fail and proposing new evaluation framework.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对生成模型日益逼真导致难以区分训练成员与生成样本的挑战，形式化提出成员与生成推理（MGI）问题，并揭示现有成员推理和归因方法在区分任务上的系统性失败。通过引入新的评估框架，为生成模型的数据隐私和真实性验证提供了重要基准。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对生成模型日益逼真导致难以区分训练成员与生成样本的挑战，形式化提出成员与生成推理（MGI）问题，并揭示现有成员推理和归因方法在区分任务上的系统性失败。通过引入新的评估框架，为生成模型的数据隐私和真实性验证提供了重要基准。
